def: s.f. Night ( = rāt).

s.f. Pleasure, enjoyment, delight, joy; desire; love, attachment (to), fondness (for), regard, favour (of); addiction (to); intentness (on); the pleasure of love, sexual passion, sexual intercourse, venery, coition; the pudenda; the goddess of love or sexual passion personified as the wife of Kām-deva; (prob. for daiv-kī ratī or rattī), the favour of fortune, fortune, destiny, luck; — a species of metre, four times ¯̆
subs: s.m. 'Ratī's husband,' Kām-deva, the god of love
(or rattī) jāgnā or ćamaknā, v.n. To begin to prosper, to thrive, flourish
s.m. Sexual intercourse, the enjoyment of carnal love
s.f. The plant Trigonella corniculata (considered, perhaps, as aphrodisiac)
(-meṅ), To be attached (to), be fond (of); to be addicted (to); to be intent (on), &c.
and rati-keli, s.f. Sexual intercourse, venery, coition
ratī-wat, ratī-want, adj. & s.m. Fortunate, prosperous, flourishing; — a fortunate or prosperous person, &c.

def: s.m. A set pension, salary, stipend, or allowance; daily allowance of food, a ration; allowance of food for dogs or elephants; dog's food

ety: for A. راتبة rātibat, fem., fr. rātib
def: s.m. Salary, stipend, pension, allowance ( = rātib).

ety: rat = rāt, 'night'+jagā = jāg(nā)+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. Watching all night (on religious occasions), a vigil.
s.m. See s.v. rata, rat.
s.m. A pound-weight (consisting of twelve ounces); a pound (of anything); a pint-measure; — a cup of wine.
wety: From Arabic رِطْل (riṭl), from Aramaic רִיטְלָא (rīṭlā), earlier לִיטְרָא (līṭrā), from Ancient Greek λίτρα (lítra).

ety: rāt˚ = Prk. रत्त(इ) or रत्ते(इ), fr. रत्त = S. रक्त, p.p.p. of rt. रञ्ज्
def: v.t. To dye, colour, tinge, stain; to dye red, &c.; — v.n. To be affected by passion, be passionately fond (of), be strongly attached (to), be deeply in love (with).

ety: raṭ˚ = S. रटय(ति), caus. of rt. रट्
def: v.t. To repeat, iterate; to call out; to solicit, or to demand, repeatedly.
s.f. Repetition, iteration; persistent solicitation or demand, importunity
subs: (-ko), To be constantly repeating; to make repeated request (for), to be importunate.

def: s.m. Jewel, gem, precious stone, pearl, treasure; the pupil of the eye; (in comp.) anything valuable or desirable, any precious thing, anything excellent or best of its kind (e.g. strī-ratn, 'a pearl of a woman')
subs: (˚na+āv˚), s.f. Row of jewels, string of pearls, necklace of gems
ratn-jaṛā, ratan-jaṛāʼū, or ratn-jaṛit, adj. Set or studded with jewels, begemmed
(or ratan-) jot, s.f. The plant Lithospermum vestitum; a kind of medicine (said to be good for the eyes)
s.m. A throne studded or adorned with precious stones
s.f. Jewel-mine, mine for precious stones
s.f. Necklace of jewels, pearl-necklace; name of a book on astronomy (and of various other works); a kind of flower
s.f. Name of a flower; — name of a Vidyādharī; — name of one of the thirty-two images supporting the throne of Vikramāditya; — a proper name
(f. -ī), and ratn-vat (f. -vatī), adj. Made or consisting of jewels; abounding in precious stones, abundantly studded or decorated with jewels
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit रत्न (ratna).

def: v.n. To be dyed red; to be affected by passion, &c. ( = rātnā, q.v.).

ety: prob. ratnā, q.v.+ ār = Prk. आलो = S. आलः
def: adj. & s.m. Red; bright, pleasant; — red colour, &c.

ety: prob. ratnā, q.v.+ ār = Prk. आलो = S. आलः
def: adj. & s.m. Red; bright, pleasant; — red colour, &c.
s.m. A kind of rice.
s.m. Night (used in comp.)
subs: (˚tra+an˚), adj. Blind at night ( = rātauṅdhā or ratauṅdhā, q.v.).

def: s.f. Night ( = rāt, q.v.)
subs: adj. & s.m. Night-wandering, night-walking, prowling or roaming at night; — night-rover, night-prowler, thief, robber; — a night-watcher, watchman, guard, patrol; — evil spirit, fiend, ghost, goblin
s.m. or rātrī-kāl, s.m., or rātrī-welā, s.f. Night-time.

def: s.f. The seed of Abrus precatorius used as a weight; a weight equal to eight barley-corns (the seed weighs about 1 5⁄16 grains Troy; the artificial rattī averages nearly 2 ¼ grains)
subs: Of the weight of a rattī, as much as a rattī; (fig.) very little.

def: s.f. The river of heaven, the celestial Ganges; — a true speech; a woman who speaks the truth.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit ऋतु (ṛtú). Doublet of رت (rut).

def: s.m. A kind of red stone hung round the neck of a child as a charm against fever and other diseases; — a red insect which destroys a wheat crop.
s.f. A night-attack; — a concubine who comes at night; — a kind of song sung by Mewātīs at night.
adj. contrac. of raudra, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رُود (rūd), from Middle Persian lwd (/⁠rōd⁠/, “river”), from Old Persian 𐎼𐎢𐎫 (r-u-t /⁠rautaʰ⁠⁠/, “river, torrent”), from Proto-Iranian *hráwtah, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *sráwtas, from Proto-Indo-European *srew-, possibly from *ser-.

def: v.t. = rauṅdnā, q.v.
v.t. = روندنا rauṅdnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Violent, impetuous, wrathful, irascible, fearful, terrible, formidable; — heat, warmth, sunshine; ardour; passion, wrath, rage.
s.f. Fierceness, horribleness, formidableness, dreadfulness.
s.f. A formidable woman; a name of Gaurī or Pārvatī.
adj. Forgiving, benign, merciful, pitiful, compassionate (see rāfat).

ety: Pehl. rokan; Zend raoghna, prob. fr. rt. rukhsh or ruć = S. रुच्
def: s.m. Fat, grease, oil, butter, clarified butter or ghī; varnish, polish; lacquer; glossiness, sleekness, smoothness or brightness, (of complexion)
subs: s.m. Oil expressed from rape or mustard-seed
s.m. A vessel for boiling butter in
s.m. An oil-merchant; a vender of butter
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رَوْغَن (rawġan).

def: adj. Greasy, oily, buttery, fried in butter
subs: Bread fried in, or baked with, butter; buttered bread.

def: adj. & s.m. Born under the asterism Rohiṇī; — the sandal-tree; sandal-wood; the Indian fig-tree; — a name of Agni; name of demon vanquished by Indra; — name of the ninth Muhūrt of the day ( = rohiṇ, q.v.).
s.m. A name of Balarām; the planet Mercury; an emerald.
s.f. = rohiṇ, q.v.
v.n. Commotion to take place; to be excited, be stirred up, be roused, be incited
subs: raulā karnā, raulā maćānā, To make a noise, to shout, to raise a commotion, cause an uproar, create a disturbance or tumult.

def: v.n. Commotion to take place; to be excited, be stirred up, be roused, be incited
subs: raulā karnā, raulā maćānā, To make a noise, to shout, to raise a commotion, cause an uproar, create a disturbance or tumult.

def: s.m. A kind of salt procured from a salt lake near the town of Sāmbhar in the Jainagar district
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian روم (rūm, “Rome; Byzantium; Asia Minor”), from Arabic رُوم (rūm), from Middle Persian 𐭧𐭫𐭥𐭬 (ḥlʿm /⁠hrōm⁠/), from Ancient Greek Ῥώμη (Rhṓmē), from Latin Rōma.

def: s.m. = raum, q.v.
s.f. = raund, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa रवण (ravaṇa), from Prakrit 𑀭𑀫𑀡 (ramaṇa), from Sanskrit रमण (rámaṇa). Cognate with Gujarati રમણ (ramaṇ).

ety: Prk. रमणअं = S. रमणीयं, rt. रम्
def: s.m. The bringing home a wife to consummate a marriage (it is a ceremony intermediate between the marriage and the gaunā, q.v.).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit रोवइ (rovaï), रोअइ (roaï), रोयइ (royaï), from Sanskrit रोदति (rodati), रोदिति (róditi).

def: conj. 'to render clear'), s.f. Lustre, water (of a sword, &c.); brightness, splendour, beauty, elegance, grace, ornament; freshness, prime; colour, complexion; flourishing state or condition; — (met.) order, symmetry
subs: raunaq-afroz honā, v.n. To grace or honour (by one's arrival or presence), to confer dignity (on); to arrive
To be in full bloom or splendour, to be in (its) prime; to be in a flourishing state
ex: اس شہر میں رونق ہی نہیں ہے۔
is śahar mẽ koī raunaq nahī̃ hai.
There is no splendour in this city.
وہ ہر محفل کی رونق ہے۔
voh har mahfil kī raunaq hai.
They are the life of every party.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رَوْنَق (rawnaq), borrowed from Arabic رَوْنَق (rawnaq). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi رونق (rvnq /⁠raunaq⁠/). Cognate with Punjabi رَوݨَق (rauṇaq).

ety: for A. رعونة, inf. n. of رعن 'to be soft,' or foolish, &c.
def: s.f. Pride, haughtiness, arrogance.
s.f. A bride brought home to consummate a marriage.
s.f. Round (as of a patrol, &c.)
s.m. Trampling, treading under foot, trampling or crushing down.

ety: prob. fr. S. प्रमृद्, or प्रमथ्; or a corr. of rūṅdhnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To trample on, to tread down; to ride over; to crush; to lay waste, to destroy; — to tread out (corn).
s.m. = rūs, ros, or rūsā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.), Royal palace, &c. ( = rāʼul, q.v.).
pron. (dialec.) = راور rāwar, راورا rāwrā, qq.v.
pron. (dialec.) = راور rāwar, راورا rāwrā, qq.v.
s.f. (colloq.) Tingling, smarting, smart; compunction, regret, remorse ( = paćhtāwā).

adj. & s.m. Made of the hide of the deer called ruru; proceeding from the ruru; — fearful, dreadful, formidable, terrible, horrible; — unsteady; fraudulent, dishonest; — a savage; — one of the divisions of the infernal regions.
s.f. A garden walk, an avenue, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رُوس (rūs), borrowed from Arabic رُوس (rūs), from Old East Slavic Русь (Rusĭ), borrowed from Proto-Finnic *roocci, borrowed from Old East Norse *rōþs-, possibly from Proto-Germanic *rōþruz, from Proto-Indo-European *h₁reh₁-.

ety: Pehl. roshan; Zend raokhshna, rt. rukhsh; S. रोचन, rt. रुच्
def: adj. Light, lighted up, alight, illuminated; bright, shining, splendid, luminous; clear, evident, manifest, conspicuous
subs: s.f. lit. 'A lighted station,' a band of musicians; a kind of serenade with pipes and small tabours
s.m. A hole for admitting light, a skylight
adj. & s.m. Of enlightened or luminous mind, enlightened, intelligent; — one of enlightened mind; one who is endowed with supernatural knowledge
adj. Of luminous mind, of splendid genius
v.t. To light (a lamp, candle, &c.); to illumine, enlighten; to bring to light, make manifest; to clear (from ambiguity)
s.m. One who makes bright or light, an illuminator; a polisher, furbisher
To be lighted, become illuminated, become bright; to be or become clear or evident (to, -par).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian روشن (rawšan).

def: s.f. Light, brightness, splendour; illumination; brightness or clearness (of vision), sight (of the eye); a lamp kept burning at a Moḥammadan tomb or shrine
subs: (-kī), To light (a lamp), to make an illumination, to illuminate.

ety: raus˚, prob. = S. रूष or रूक्ष; ˚tī = S. इता; — ˚al and alī = Prk. अलो and अलिआ = S. अ+लः and ल+इका
def: A light loamy soil.
s.m. (dialec.), 'Royal messenger'; a land-bailiff, a person employed to collect rent from a village, an agent (syn. mahto); a trooper, cavalier.
s.m. = rāʼetā, q.v.
s.f. Chiefship, &c.

ety: for A. روضة, fr. روض 'to break, or train,' &c.
def: s.m. A beautiful garden, a garden; the tomb of a learned or pious man, a mausoleum
subs: s.m. One who recites the praises of Husain in the month Moharram; a eulogist at the tomb of a saint or beloved person.

ety: Pehl. roćan; Zend raoćana, rt. ruć; S. रोचन, rt. रुच्
def: s.m. An aperture in the middle of a house (for allowing the smoke to escape), a hole; a window.
ex:
ہو گئے ہیں جمع اجزائے نگاہِ آفتاب
ذرّے اس کے گھر کی دیواروں کے روزن میں نہیں
ho gae ha͠i jamā ajzā-e nigāh-e āftāb
zarre us ke ghar kī dīvārõ ke rauzan mẽ nahī̃

The parts of the gaze of the sun have become collected
There are no dust grains in the crevices of the walls of her home
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian روزن (rawzan, rōzan).

def: s.m. Cry, clamour, din; sound, noise; voice; hum; report, fame.
s.f. Current, stream, torrent; line, train; host, swarm; vehemence, impetuosity, passion.

ety: act. part. of raftan; rt. Zend harp or hrap? = S. सर्प् (सृप्)
def: part. adj. Going (used in comp., as pesh-raw, 'Going before')
subs: vulg. raw-ā-rau, and raw-ā-raw-ī, vulg. rau-ā-rau-ī, s.f. Going, proceeding, travelling, following quick; quick movement, haste, hurrying, hurry.

ety: part. n. of raftan; see raw
def: part. adj. Going; flowing; passing; current; lawful, right; equitable, reasonable; admissible, allowable; approved, tolerated; worthy of reception or attention; worthy, proper, right, suitable (syn. jāʼiz)
subs: v.t. To deem right, to think fit or proper; to approve
adj. & s.m. Judging or holding to be right, approving, lawful, &c. ( = rawā); — approver, chooser, consenter; — rawā-dār honā (-kā), To deem right; to permit (oneself) to do
v.t. To hold, esteem, or consider lawful, right, or proper; to uphold, maintain, justify: to receive, acquiesce in; — to make current, or legal, &c.; to warrant
v.t. To make going; to cause to flow; to supply, render obtainable.

def: s.m. A grain (of sand, dust, gunpowder, &c.); a granule (of precious metal, or of congealed honey, or ghī, &c.); granulous wheaten flour; grit (as in meal, &c.); a particle (of gold or silver), filings (of precious metals); a lump or little mass; a bit, piece, a little.
s.m. pl. (of rāfiẓ, q.v.), Heretics, (partilarly) Shīʻas (so called from their forsaking Zaid ibn ʻAlī ibnuʼl-Ḥusain).
s.m. = rawīya or rawaiya, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of روج 'to have a ready sale,' &c.
def: s.m. Being vendible, being in great demand; being current; — currency, prevalence; usage, use, custom, practice, fashion; — vent, sale; — adj. Saleable, vendible; current, customary
subs: v.n. To obtain currency, pass current; to be customary; to become prevalent; to be in force; to be in fashion; to have effect
v.n. To become current or customary, to prevail
(-ko), To give currency (to), to make current, make customary; to introduce; to publish, spread abroad

def: adj. Granulous, composed of granules or minute and distinct particles (as honey, ghī, &c.); — s.m. A grain or particle of silver or gold ( = rawā).

ety: Prk. राउलं, or राअउलं; S. राज+कुलं
def: s.m. (dialec.), Prince; chieftain, chief; warrior, soldier; — court of a king, royal palace ( = rāʼur).
adj. Royal, princely; — knightly, soldier-like.

s.m. lit. 'The Vociferator'; name of the ruler of Lankā or Ceylon, and the famous chief of the Rākshasas or demons whose subjugation and destruction by Rāmćandra, the seventh incarnation of Vishṇu, form the subject of the epic poem called Rāmāyaṇ, (he was the son of Viśravas by Kaikasī, and the half-brother of Kuvera).

ety: Prk. रमणो, or रवँणु?; S. रमणः
def: s.m. Husband; lover, sweetheart, gallant; — raun, s.m. = raunā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa रवण (ravaṇa), from Prakrit 𑀭𑀫𑀡 (ramaṇa), from Sanskrit रमण (rámaṇa). Cognate with Gujarati રમણ (ramaṇ).

ety: prop. ruwān; old P. ruãn, rvãn; Pehl. rubān; Zend urvan
def: s.m. The rational soul; soul, spirit, life.
wety: From Classical Persian روان (ravān, “fluid, flowing”), a compound of رو (rav) + ـان (-ân); the former is the present stem of رفتن (raftan).

def: adj. Going, passing, moving, running, current; flowing, fluid, fluent, running or flowing smoothly; quick, brisk, active, sharp, expert, dexterous; — s.m. Text; reading; — adv. Fluently, smoothly; quickly, briskly, &c.
subs: v.n. To go running about, to wander to and fro, to stroll up and down
v.t. To set agoing; to make current; to cause to flow, to spill, shed; to supply ( = rawā karnā); to send, despatch, forward; to impart briskness to, make active or quick; to sharpen, whet.
wety: From Classical Persian روان (ravān, “fluid, flowing”), a compound of رو (rav) + ـان (-ân); the former is the present stem of رفتن (raftan).

def: adj. & s.m. = روانه rawāna, q.v.
adj. Going, &c. ( = rawān); departed; — despatched, sent; — s.m. A custom-house passport, a pass, permit
subs: v.n. To be flowing, to flow; to be in motion, be proceeding; to proceed; to depart, to set out, to start (on a journey); to depart this life, to die; to be despatched, be sent.

def: s.f. Running, flowing, flux; going, passing; travelling, setting out, departure; despatch, forwarding (of a letter, &c.); — purport of a pass, or passport, &c.
subs: s.f. A pass, passport; port clearance.

def: s.m. = ramaṇak, q.v.
s.m. A filter made of cane or bamboo.
s.f. corr. of raunaq, q.v.
s.f. Rhubarb (root of the plant called rebās, q.v.)

ety: raw, q.v.+Zend aff. añṭ = S. अन्त्
def: part. (of raftan) & s.m. Going, travelling; — goer, traveller, passenger.
s.m. Name of Indra-jit, the eldest son of the demon Rāvan.
s.f. Going, proceeding; course; running; currency; flowing, flow, flux; effusion; fluency; reading; briskness (of market).
s.f. = ramaṇī, q.v.
s.m. = رونه rawanna, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit रोवइ (rovaï), रोअइ (roaï), रोयइ (royaï), from Sanskrit रोदति (rodati), रोदिति (róditi).

ety: corr. of P. rawanda, or rawāna
def: s.m. A pass, permit, a passport; — a servant who attends at the gate of the women's apartments to purchase articles that are required; an errand-boy.

ety: S. रोमशः; or rawāṅ = roʼāṅ, 'hair'+s = sā; cf. roʼāṅsā below
def: s.m. A kind of bean, Dolichos sinensis.
adj. = roʼāsā, q.v.

r: rāwar, rāʼur, rāʼorā, rāʼurā, rāʼoro, rāʼorau
def: pron. (f. -ī), Of you, yours ( = bumhārā, q.v.).
s.f. See s.v. raw, 'going.'
s.f. (dialec.) = rawāṅs, q.v.
adj. (f. ravantī), Crying out, raising a cry, bellowing.
s.m. Prince; chieftain, chief; hero, warrior; — a high caste of sweepers (who do not eat the leavings of others, as sweepers commonly do); an individual of that caste (cf. měh-tar).
s.m. (in Bundelkhanḍ), Bread made of kodoṅ or sāṅwāṅ.
s.m. The Indian cuckoo, the Koʼil.
s.m. pl. (of rātib, q.v.), Necessaries of life, &c.
s.m. The sun
subs: s.f. 'Daughter of the sun' ( = ravi-nanḍinī, q.v.)
s.m. A sort of crystal, sun-stone; — ray of the sun
s.m. Name of an old Hindū commentator
s.f. 'Daughter of the sun,' an epithet of the river Jamnā — ravi-vār, vulg. rabi-bār, s.m. Sunday (syn. itwār).

ety: for A. راوٍ rāwiṉ, act. part. of روي 'to bring water'; 'to transmit orally,' &c.
def: s.m. Relater, reciter, rehearser, narrator, historian; author of a fable, romance, or fiction, &c. (Pers. pl. rāwiyān; Ar. pl. (obl.) rāwiyīn, Relaters, &c.).

ety: rav, q.v.+aff. ish = Zend shi or she
def: s.f. Motion, walk, gait, carriage; practice, custom, fashion, usage; rule, institution, law; conduct, behaviour; order, course, proceeding, procedure; manner, method, mode, way; — a garden-walk, path, avenue, passage, gallery.
s.m. Custom, practice, fashion, way, manner, behaviour, conduct; rule, law, institution (syn. ćalan).

ety: raw˚ = Prk. रव(इ), fr. S. रव, rt. रु; or S. रौ(ति), rt. रु
def: v.n. To give forth sound, to sound, to make a noise; — s.m. A thing that gives forth sound (as a rattle, &c.); small pebbles or shot, &c. inserted in anything to produce a sound or noise.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit रोवइ (rovaï), रोअइ (roaï), रोयइ (royaï), from Sanskrit रोदति (rodati), रोदिति (róditi).

def: s.m. A cloth spread upon a bed, &c. (for a person to lie upon).
s.m. Stream (of a river), current; rapid flow, velocity, speed, swiftness.

ety: for رأيٌ, inf. n. of رأي 'to see; to suppose,' &c.
def: s.f. Belief, judgment, opinion, notion, thought, meditation, deliberation; wisdom, intelligence, skill in affairs; counsel, advice
subs: (-kī), To ask the opinion (of), seek the advice (of), to consult
(-kī), To accept the opinion (of), to submit or bow to the judgment (of)
To give an opinion; to vote
s.m. Expression of opinion; a communication.

def: s.m. Prince; chief; a Hindū title (cf. rāʼo); (in comp. as first member) any principal object, anything the best or largest of its kind
subs: s.f. A species of jasmine, Jasminum zambac
s.f. A species of the jamblong tree, Calyptranthes caryophyllifolia
s.m. 'Prince of princes,' a title among Hindūs; the Dīwān of the Khālisa or chief treasurer
s.m. A prince's son, a young prince
(or munī), rāʼe-maniyā, s.m. A kind of rice (in the husk)
s.m. The lāl or male bird of the Fringilla amandava (see muniyā).

def: s.m. = rājā, and rāy, qq.v.
s.m. Wine; — afternoon.

r: rayā, rāy, rāʼi, rāyā, rahā, rahnā, raynā
def: 1˚ = rāy or rāʼi; 2˚ = rāyā; 3˚ = rahā, perf. part. of rahnā (cf. raynā).
s.m. pl. (of raiḥān), Sweet-smelling plants, odoriferous herbs.
s.f. (dialec.) = rain, q.v

ety: for A. راية, v.n. fr. رأي 'to see'
def: s.f. Banner, standard (of an army).
s.f. pl. (of rāyat, q.v.), Banners, standards (esp. of an army in motion); (met.) a camp.
v.n. corr. of rahnā, q.v.

ety: Pehl. rāj; Zend razaṅh, rt. raz; S. रहस्, rt. रह्
def: s.m. A secret, mystery
subs: s.m. One who keeps or conceals a secret

adj. & s.m. Trusty, faithful, who keeps a secret; — one who keeps a secret; one who is intrusted with a secret, a confidant; one who is cognizant of, or privy to, a criminal act or offence, an accessory (to a fact)
s.f. Keeping of secrets, secrecy; the keeping secret, having knowledge of a criminal act and not giving information
adj. & s.m. Acquainted with secrets or mysteries; — one who is acquainted with a secret, &c.
To divulge a secret, to betray the secrets (of), to expose
rāz-ě-nihufta, s.m. A hidden or close secret; a private matter
or rāz-o-niyāz, s.m. Secret conversation or intercourse (as that of prayer); lover's secrets, loving prattle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رَاز (rāz). First attested in c. 1636 as Middle Hindi راز (raz).

def: s.m. A builder, mason, bricklayer ( = rāj, q.v.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رَاز (rāz). First attested in c. 1636 as Middle Hindi راز (raz).

def: s.f. A vineyard; a vine; a grape; — battle (cf. razm); — a castle in Medina.

(inf. n. of رضع 'to suck a mother's breast'), s.m. = P رضاعت raẓāʻat (for A. رضاعة, inf. n. of رضع), s.f. Sucking; fosterage; — (Mohammadan law) a bond of affinity between persons nursed by the same female, analogous to consanguinity.

ety: prop. riẓā, v.n. fr. رضو 'to be pleased,' &c.
def: s.f. The state of being pleased, content, &c.; pleasure, good pleasure, will; content; approval; acquiescence, consent, assent; permission, leave, furlough
subs: s.f. List of holy-days, feasts, &c. in the course of the year (on which days public offices are closed)
s.m. One who seeks the goodwill or pleasure, or the gratification (of another)
s.f. Seeking the goodwill or pleasure or approbation (of), effort to please
adj. Willing, wishing, consenting, acquiescing, compliant; permitting (cf. rāẓī)
s.f. Good pleasure, willingness; consent; satisfaction; compliance; permission
s.f. Free will and pleasure, free consent
adv. Willingly, of (one's) free will and consent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رضا (rizā), from Arabic رِضَا (riḍā).

def: (for A. رضاعة, inf. n. of رضع), s.f. = A رضاع raẓāʻ (inf. n. of رضع 'to suck a mother's breast'), s.m. Sucking; fosterage; — (Mohammadan law) a bond of affinity between persons nursed by the same female, analogous to consanguinity.

ety: = S. रञ्जिक+इका, prob. through the P. razāʼī, fr. razīdan, rt. raz = S. रञ्ज्
def: s.f. A quilt, a coloured coverlet.
adj. Sucking, foster (used in comp.)

ety: for A. rāẓiṉ, act. part. of رضو 'to be pleased,' &c.
def: adj. Pleased, well-pleased, content, contented, satisfied, agreed, willing, acquiescent; regarding with good will or favour, liking, approving; (colloq.) well, in good health
subs: (or raẓā), adj. Resigned to the will of God, resigned, contented; — God willing
v.t. To make contented or satisfied by chastising, to chastise ( = ṭhīk banānā)
adj. Contented and happy; well and happy
adv. With the greatest pleasure, willingly, gladly
v.t. To please, gratify; to content, satisfy; to win over, persuade, prevail upon; to reconcile, conciliate
s.m. A deed of agreement or compromise by which a plaintiff or prosecutor acknowledges that he has been satisfied by the defendant; an agreement, a written consent

v.n. To be pleased (with, -se), to approve (of), to regard with favour; to be satisfied or content (with, -se or -par), to be willing, to agree or consent (to, -par), to assent (to), acquiesce (in), comply (with), accede (to); to condescend, deign.

def: adj. Of or relating to a secret, or to secrecy.

ety: act. part. n. fr. رزق 'to grant the means of subsistence'
def: s.m. The supplier of the means of subsistence (rizq, or arzāq), sustainer, an epithet of the Deity.
adj. = riẕāla, q.v.
adv. Contentedly, to (one's) content or satisfaction (rare).

ety: Zend raśman, fr. arej, 'to draw out,' = S. अर्ज् (ऋञ्जति)
def: s.m. War, battle, combat

ety: intens. n. fr. رزق 'to give subsistence,' &c.
def: s.m. The Supplier of the means of subsistence, the Giver of daily bread, &c. to his creatures, Providence (an epithet applied to God alone).

ety: intens. n. fr. رزق; see rāziq
def: s.m. The great Giver of the means of subsistence, an epithet of the Deity.
s.f. The giving the means of subsistence, &c., an attribute of the Deity.

r: re, ra, rāʼe mǒhmala, rāʼe manqūt̤a, r, ṛ, l, abjad, rabīʻuʼl-awwal, ra-kār
ety: called, in Urdū, rāʼe mǒhmala, and rāʼe manqūt̤a
def: s.m. The letter or sound ra.

part. O! ho there! holloa! fellow! good fellow! friend! oh! alas! bravo! (used after the object addressed, whereas are is used before it, — and expressive of contempt or disrespect, of familiarity or endearment, of admiration, and of woe).
adj. & s.m. corr. of rev, q.v.
s.m. Sorrel; the plant of which rhubarb is the root, Rheum ribes, or R. palmatum (cf. rāwand; rewand).

ety: fr. Zend rić = S. रिच्; cf. rez, reza, rekhta, and rīḵẖ
def: s.f. Sand (syn. ret)
subs: s.f. Moving or shifting sand, quicksand; sand agitated as waves (by the wind)
reg-istān, s.m. Sandy region or tract, sandy desert, sandy place
s.f. Rubbing or polishing with sand or sand-paper
s.f. lit. 'Sand-fish'; the skink, Lacerta scincus (used, in a dried state, in medicine).

def: adj. (local), Thin, dry, lean; — s.m. A lean beast.
v.t. corr. of reṅgānā, q.v.
= H ريگڙ रेगड़ regaṛ (see reghārī), s.f.(?) Black soil.
= H ريگر रेगर regar (see reghārī), s.f.(?) Black soil.
s.f. = reghārī, q.v.
s.f. A furrow.
s.m. (dialec.) = rekhrū, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. reg.

ety: Prk. रेहा or रेही, fr. रेह् = S. रेज्; cf. Pers. rīh
def: s.f. Fossil alkali (used for washing and making soap), impure nitrate of soda, fuller's earth (it abounds in some soils as an efflorescence, rendering them altogether unproductive); — brackish or barren soil.
adj. Impregnated with alkali (said of soil), brackish, barren.
adj. Impregnated with alkali (said of soil), brackish, barren.
s.f. = reh, q.v.
s.m. = rahlā, q.v.

ety: for A. رحلة, inf. n. of un. fr. رحل; see raḥl
def: s.f. Removal, departure, journey; departure from this life, death, decease
subs: v.n. To take (one's) departure; to depart this life, to die.

def: v.t. To mark with lines, to streak, to delineate.

ety: reh, q.v.+ṛī = aṛī = Prk. अडिआ = S. अ+र+इका
def: s.f. Sandy or barren soil, brackisk land or soil.
(i.q. rahṛū, q.v.), A cart, sled, sledge.
s.f. = rahū, rohū, q.v.
s.f. Abundance, &c. ( = rel-pel, q.v.).
s.f. = rekhān, q.v.
s.f. = ريکهہ rekh, q.v.
s.f. A line, &c. ( = rekhā, q.v.); a black line (made with missī) on the teeth
subs: The whiskers, &c. to begin to appear, to have down on the cheek
or rekh-rūp, s.m. Lineaments and complexion, features and countenance, shape and form, the contour and the tout-ensemble.

def: s.f. Line; streak, stripe; row, series; mark; writing; a furrow; lines on the palm of the hand; fate, destiny; (in Astron.), the first meridian, the line drawn from Lankā (Ceylon) to Meru, i.e. from the equator to the north pole; fulness, satisfaction; — deceit, fraud; — a small quantity, a little
s.f. Land that is beyond the reach of river water.
s.m. See s.v. rekh.

ety: perf. part. of reḵẖtan, rt. Zend rić = S. रिच्; cf. S. रेचित
def: part. adj. Poured out; scattered; mixed; — s.m. 'The mixed dialect,' the Hindūstānī or Urdū language (as used by men, cf. reḵẖtī); — a Hindūstānī ode; — mortar, plaster.
s.f. Hindūstānī verse written in the language of women, and expressing the sentiments, &c. peculiar to them. (The two principal writers in this idiom are the poets Rangīn and Jān Sāḥib.)
s.m. Rail, railway
s.f. Crowd; abundance, &c. (see rel-pel, the more com. form)
subs: s.f. A continuous line (of people, &c.), crowd, throng, press, crush (cf. rewaṛ); abundance, profusion, exuberance, glut (in a market)
To crowd, throng; to press, push, jostle; to supply in abundance, to overstock, to glut (a market)

def: s.m. A line or string of animals; a rushing stream, a torrent, a flood, inundation; — rushing, pushing, rush, push, press, crush, shove, assault
subs: (-ko), To give a push, to push, &c. ( = relnā, q.v.).

ety: prob. fr. S. प्रेर्; cf. pelnā
def: v.t. To push, shove, jostle, hustle.
s.m. = رينو reṇu, q.v.
s.f. The Palma christi or castor-oil plant (the more common form is renṛ; — see reṅḍī).
s.f. The Palma christi, Ricinus vulgaris ( = reṅḍ or reṅṛ); — the berry or seed of the Palma christi

ety: prob. reṅd, dialec. for retī, and reṅṭ, qq.v.+S. aff. इका or ईया
def: s.f. A small melon (syn. ḵẖarbuza).
s.m. corr. of رينو reṇu, q.v.
s.f. Creeping, crawling.
v.t. To cause to creep or crawl, &c.
s.f. Sciatica, hipgout; spasm (of a limb); — the plant Solanum jacquini (syn. bhaṭ-kaṭaiyā).

ety: reṅk(nā)+ṭ = Prk. aḍa = S. ṭ+ā = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. (f. -ī), A young ass, foal of an ass.
s.f. Braying (of an ass).

ety: reṅk˚ = Prk. रेक्क(इ), or रेक्के(इ), fr. S. रेष् (acc. sing. neut. रेट्)+कृ
def: v.n. To bray (as an ass); — to bellow, low (as a buffalo, &c.).
s.f. = renḍ, q.v.
s.f. Constant crying or whimpering (of a child); scraping or discordant sounds (of a stringed instrument).
s.m. Vehicle, carriage, cart.
s.f. = reṅḍī, q.v.
( = reṅḍu), s.m. corr. of رينو reṇu, q.v.
s.m. corr. of rahaṭ, q.v.
s.f. Mucus (from the nose), snot, snivel (syn. poṅṭā; — cf. rīm).
s.m. The fruit of the theswā tree.
s.m. contrac. of reṅgṭā, q.v.
s.f. Mucus (from the nose), snot, snivel (syn. poṅṭā; — cf. rīm).
s.m.f. Dust; sand; powder; a grain of dust or sand, &c.; an atom; grit; pollen (of flowers).

s.f. Name of a fragrant drug or medicinal substance (of a bitter and slightly pungent taste and greyish colour: it is procured in grains about the size of pepper-corns); — a species of pulse, Ervum; — name of the wife of Jamad-agni and mother of Paraśu-rām.
s.f. (dialec.) = rew, q.v.
s.m. The letter र r; the mark (?) which represents the letter r when it is placed over a following consonant.
s.m. corr. of resham, q.v.

ety: Pehl. resh; Zend raêsha, rt. rish; S. रेष, rt. रिष्, (रेषति)
def: s.m. Wound; sore; scar; pus, matter; — (in comp.) wounding, wounded (e.g. dil-resh, 'wounding, or wounded in, the heart').

ety: prob. Zend raêsha; cf. rīsh; and resmān
def: s.m. Fibre; filament; nerve; vein (of a leaf); stringiness (of a mango, &c.)
s.m. Silk (thread, or cloth)

ety: resham, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Silken, made or woven of silk.

ety: resham, q.v.+Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Silken, made or woven of silk.

ety: res or rīs, fr. ristan, 'to spin,' &c., rt. Zend rīć = S. रिच्+mān = Zend man = S. मन्; cf. S. रश्मन्
def: s.f. String, cord, thread, rope ( = rassī, q.v.).
adj. = reshmī, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.), Envy; emulation ( = rīs, q.v.).
s.f. Sand; filings.
s.m. Sand; filings ( = ret).
s.m. = retas, q.v.
s.m. = retas, q.v.
s.f. Price paid for filing, &c. (see retnā).

ety: ret, q.v.+al = Prk. अइल्लो = S. इलः
def: adj. Abounding in sand, sandy, gravelly; gritty; — s.f. Sandy ground or soil, sand ( = retlī, q.v.).
s.m. = retas, q.v.
s.m. Seminal fluid, semen, sperm, sperma genitale; seed, offspring; quicksilver (regarded as Śiva's semen).
s.f. Sandy ground on the bank of a river, a tract of alluvial land.
s.f. A file ( = retnī).
adj. (f. -ī), Sandy; gravelly; gritty ( = retal).

ety: retī+ā = āw = Prk. āwa or āwe = S. āpi (caus. augment); nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To file, rasp; to polish.

ety: for retilā = ret+Prk. अइल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī) = retal, q.v.
s.f. Sandy ground or soil; a tract of alluvial land ( = retī).
v.t. To file, rasp; to polish; to thrum.
s.f. A file ( = retī).
s.m. A filer; a polisher.
s.f. Trick, fraud, deceit, guile.

ety: prob. S. रेणु; or S. रेवा, rt. रेव्
def: s.f. (dialec.) Sand, fine gravel; grit; grain (of sand, sugar, &c.); a shoal, a ford (cf. rewṛī).
s.f. Name of the Narmadā river (which rises in one of the Vindhya mountains called Amarkanṭak, in the province of Gondwānā); — name of Rati, wife of Kām-deva.
corr. of rewand, q.v.: — rewan-ćīnī = rewand-ćīnī.
s.f. (m. ?) Rhubarb, Rheum ribes or R. emodi ( = rāwand)
s.f. The flower Jasminum angustifolium.

ety: prob. S. रेव (rt. रेव्)+ṛ = Prk. डी = S. र (or ट)+ई; — cf. rel, relā
def: s.f. A continuous line or string (of animals); a herd or flock (of goats or sheep).
s.m. = rebās, q.v.
s.m. Name of an ancient king, father of Revatī, and father-in-law of Balarām.

s.f. Name of a daughter of Revat and wife of Balarām; name of the twenty-seventh (or the fifth) Nakshatra or lunar asterism (consisting of Piscium ζ and thirty-one other stars, and figured by a tabor); — name of one of the female energies (mātr̤ī) of the gods

ety: S. रेव+ṛī = Prk. डिआ = र (or ट)+इका
def: s.f. A kind of sweetmeat (a small cake of solidified sugar covered with sesamum-seeds)
subs: 'To fall into the complication of the reʼoṛī'; to be involved in difficulties, to find oneself in a difficulty, to be disconcerted or discomfited (the allusion is to a game founded on the principle of geometrical progression, where one bets that he will eat the sum of the reoṛīs obtained by doubling one reoṛī a certain number of times, and is confounded at finding it amount to a quantity far exceeding his expectation).

ety: fr. reḵẖtan, imperat. rez; rt. Zend rić = S. रिच्
def: part. Pouring, scattering, dropping, shedding, infusing, applying, &c. (used in comp., e.g. ashk-rez, 'shedding tears'; dur-rez, 'scattering pearls'; rang-rez, 'a painter'); — s.f. A small thing, anything crumbled or reduced to powder; a grain, scrap, bit, a crumb ( = reza), a drop, draught; — a favour, mercy; — profusion, abundance, affluence, wealth; — a cup, goblet, bowl; — will, desire, wish, concupiscence
subs: v.t. To pour out or forth, to scatter, to shed; to begin to chirp or talk (as young birds).

r: reza, rez, lit, rezgī, thān, reza-ćīn
def: adj. & s.m. Picking up scraps; — one who picks up scraps
subs: s.f. The picking up of scraps
adj. & s.m. Eating scraps or remnants (of food); — one who eats scraps, &c.
adj. & adv. In bits, in pieces, broken in pieces, small; — s.m. Scraps, pieces
v.t. To break in pieces, to smash, crush, shatter, shiver
v.n. To be broken in pieces, to be smashed, to be pounded small.

r: rezagī, rezgī, rezak, reza, ī, i
ety: rezak = reza+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. A scrap, bit, piece ( = reza); small coin (as two-anna, four-anna, or eight-anna pieces), change; a small piece of gold or silver wire; a child employed in mason-work ( = reza, q.v.).

ety: imperf. part. of reḵẖtan; see rez
def: part. Pouring, scattering, shedding, dropping (used as last member of compounds).
s.f. = rezgī, q.v.

ety: rez, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Pouring, scattering, &c. (see rez; — used in comp., e.g. ḵẖūn-rezī, 'blood-shedding).

ety: rez, q.v.+ish = Zend shi or she
def: s.f. Pouring out, scattering; flowing in small quantities, running; a running at the nose.

ety: for A. رعاية, inf. n. of رعي 'to pasture, to tend'
def: s.f. Guarding, protecting, tending, looking (after), taking care (of, -kī), paying attention (to); observance, respect, regard, attention, honour; kindness, favour, patronage, partiality; indulgence, remission, mitigation, abatement; pity, clemency, leniency; — a form of writing in which the ideas introduced correspond with that suggested by the first word (e.g. the word 'garden' requires that the images in the sentences following should be 'verdure,' 'flowers,' 'birds,' &c.)
subs: (-kī), To make observance (of), be careful, take care (of); to pay regard or heed or attention (to); to show favour or indulgence (to), to be partial (to), be lenient, be indulgent; to remit, to mitigate.

def: adj. & s.m. Favoured, privileged; granted as a favour or indulgence, privilege (leave); — a favoured or privileged person, a protégé; one to whom remission or abatement has been granted.
s.m. Rope, fastening, ligament; a building for the accommodation of travellers and their beasts, an inn, a caravansary; a hospice; a religious house (pl. ribāt̤āt).
s.m. The keeper of a ribāt̤, innkeeper, &c.
s.m. (f. -in, or -nī) = next, q.v.

s.f. Lustre, splendour; — a hymn; a single verse, stanza, or text; — the Rig-veda (so called because it consists chiefly of hymns to the Hindū deities); a verse or text from the Rig-veda; a mystical verse or text from a Veda, magical invocation.

s.f. Lustre, splendour; — a hymn; a single verse, stanza, or text; — the Rig-veda (so called because it consists chiefly of hymns to the Hindū deities); a verse or text from the Rig-veda; a mystical verse or text from a Veda, magical invocation.
s.m. corr. of r̤ićīk, q.v.
s.m. = r̤iksha; and rīćh, qq.v.
s.m. corr. of riksheś, q.v.s.v. r̤iksha.
s.m. (f. -in, or -nī), A bear
s.m. Name of a saint, the father of Jamadagni; — name of a country.

s.f. A cloak or mantle (worn by Dervishes); a cloak or wrapper (put on when going out); an upper garment of fine linen unsewn or in one piece (or sewn, if made of other cloth) reaching to the middle of the leg.
adj. & s.m. Increased; prosperous, thriving, rising; — a prosperous person, &c.

s.f. Increase, growth; plenty; affluence, wealth, property; success, prosperity, good fortune; accomplishment, perfection; — name of a medicinal plant; prosperity personified as the wife of Kuver; name of the goddess Pārvatī

ety: prob. pl. of rafhuṉ, inf. n. of رفه; used in Persian as a sing.
def: s.f. An easy or comfortable state of life, enjoyment of life, ease, comfort, affluence; content, repose, quiet, tranquillity, relief.
(inf. n. of رفق 'to be gentle, kind, or courteous,' &c.), s.m.? Gentleness, tenderness, kindness, graciousness, benignity, benevolence, courtesy, civility.
s.m. The persuasion, or creed, of a rāfiẓa or heretic; heresy, schism; — (corr. of A. rafẓ, inf. n. of رفض), abandoning, leaving, deserting.
s.f. = r̤ik; and r̤ig-veda or r̤ig-bed, q.v.s.v r̤ik.
s.m. (local), contrac. of registān, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. r̤ik.

ety: inf. n. of روح 'to be windy,' &c.
def: s.f. Wind, air; flatus, flatulence; puff, whiff; odour, exhalation
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ریح (rīh), from Arabic ريح (rīḥ).

ety: inf. n. of iii of رهن 'to remain,' &c.
def: s.m. Betting on a horse-race.
s.m. pl. (of rahn or rihn), Pledges; bets, stakes.
adj. Windy; flatulent.

s.m. An invisible being which moves in a circular orbit round the world (syn. yoginī. On the 1st, 9th, 16th, and 24th days of a lunar month, his station is in the east; on the 3rd, 11th, 18th, and 26th, in the south-east; on the 5th, 13th, 20th, and 28th, in the south; on the 4th, 12th, 19th, and 27th, in the south-west; on the 6th, 14th, 21st, and 29th, in the west; on the 7th, 15th, and 22nd in the north-west; on the 2nd, 10th, 17th, and 25th, in the north; and on the 8th, 23rd, and 30th, in the north-east. His influence on each day is especially exerted during nine ghaṛīs, or three hours thirty-six minutes, at the close of that tith or lunar day; and, in that interval, it is unfortunate to begin a journey towards the station of this being, or in such a direction as to place him on the right hand).
adj. corr. of رزاله rizāla, q.v.
s.f. Pleasing, satisfying; pleasure, satisfaction, gratification; desire, wish, inclination; love, liking; approbation
subs: s.m. One who conceals his desire or inclination, &c.

def: v.n. (prov.) = rījhnā, q.v.
v.t. To ravish, enchant, charm, delight, please; to excite, annoy, vex, tease, plague, perplex.
v.n. To be rejoiced, be pleased, be delighted (with, -se), to be gratified.
adj. Having the quality of being pleased; easily pleased or gratified.
adj. = rijhwār, q.v.

ety: rījh(nā), q.v.+wār = wāl or wālā; and ˚waiyā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: adj. Having the quality of being pleased; easily pleased or gratified.

ety: rījh(nā), q.v.+wār = wāl or wālā; and ˚waiyā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: adj. Having the quality of being pleased; easily pleased or gratified.
adj. Straight, right; upright, honest, sincere
s.f. = S رجتو ऋजुत्व r̤ijutva, s.m. Straightness; rectitude, honesty.
s.m. = S رجتا ऋजुता r̤ijutā, s.f. Straightness; rectitude, honesty.
s.f. A verse of the Rig-veda; — the Rig-veda
subs: s.m. The constellation of the Bear
vulg. r̤ig-bed, s.m. The Rig-veda, the first and most important of the four Vedas (so called because it consists chiefly of hymns or sacred verses in praise of the Hindū deities).

def: s.m. pl. (of ruqʻa), Notes, &c.; — (in Pers., as also riqāʻī), sing. A kind of handwriting.

ety: v.n. of رکب 'to ride, mount,' &c.; — used in Persian with amplification of signification
def: s.f. A stirrup; — train, equipage, retinue, cavalcade; — a catch word at the bottom of a page (to indicate the first word of the following page); — a tall and eight-sided goblet, a goblet, bowl; a dish, plate ( = rikābī)
subs: s.m. A stirrup-holder, a servant who runs at the stirrup (of a great man), an attendant, a groom; — a cup-bearer; — keeper of the dishes, pantry-man, steward, butler; — a manufacturer and vender of preserves, pickies, and the like
adv. 'With foot in stirrup,' ready to start
adv. In attendance (on, -ke); in company (with), along (with).

def: s.m. pl. (of raqaba), Necks; slaves, servants.
s.f. A broad flat dish, a plate, a copper saucer; — a drinking cup, a bowl; — a kind of rupee current in Lakhnau
s.f. A dish, plate (see rikābī).

ety: rt. Zend rić = S. रिच्; cf. S. रेक; and see rekh-ta
def: s.f. Purging; thin excrement in diarrhœa
s.m. (Gaṛhwālī) = rīćh, q.v.
s.m. corr. of r̤ishi, q.v. (cf. also rikhī).
s.m. A sage, saint, &c. (see r̤ishi)

ety: for A. رقة, inf. n. of رقّ 'to be thin,' &c.
def: s.f. Thinness, attenuated state or condition, attenuation, flimsiness, want of consistency; minuteness, subtileness; — tender-heartedness, mercy, compassion, pity, sympathy; affection; softness; — weeping; — ecstasy, frenzy, religious transport, enthusiasm.
s.m. (f. -ī), A bear (vulg. rīćh); — a star; a constellation.
part. adj. & s.m. Emptied, cleared; empty, void, destitute (of); — a vacuity, vacuum; — a wood, forest
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit रक्त (rakta).

def: s.f. Emptiness, vacuity, void. empty space, space.

ety: Zend iri or ri = S. री+Zend aff. man = S. मन्
def: s.f. Pus, matter, humour, rheum; — dregs, dross, lees (of wine).
adj. Mattery, purulent, suppurative
subs: s.f. Piles with a white or pale mucous discharge.

def: adv. With a pattering sound, patter-patter (as rain).
s.m. An obligation; debt; — (in Alg.) a negative quantity, minus
subs: s.m. The clearing off a debt
adj. & s.m. Borrowing; — a borrower, one who contracts debts; — r̤iṇ-grasta, part. adj. Indebted, involved in debt
To take a loan, to borrow
s.m. One who does not pay his debts
s.f. = r̤iṇ-moksh, s.m. Discharge of a debt; freedom from debt.

def: s.m. = rīćh, q.v.

ety: prob. connec. with S. रद् or रन्ध; cf. randa
def: s.m. A sceptic; a knave, rogue; a lewd fellow, reprobate, drunkard, debauchee, blackguard, profligate, libertine, rake (pl. rindān).

ety: abst. s. fr. P. rindak, or rinda, fr. rind, q.v.
def: s.f. Dissoluteness, lewdness, profligacy, debauchery, fraud, &c. ( = rindī).
adj. Dissolute, lewd, disorderly, licentious, profligate, rakish, like a debauchee.
v.t. To dress (victuals), to cook, to boil.
s.f. Trick, fraud; lewdness, debauchery.
v.n. = reṅknā, q.v.
